title: FX Transfer Use Case — Dual Amount Entry and Validation
description: Update SaveTransferUseCase to support cross-currency transfers. When source and target accounts have different currencies, the caller must provide targetAmountCents. Validate that FX transfers always have both amounts set.
execution_steps: Run 'loom start TASK-117' to lock active context
Run 'loom context TASK-117' to ingest requirements
Write failing tests for SaveTransferUseCase (Red Phase):
- Same-currency transfer: one amount, succeeds; targetAmountCents remains null
- FX transfer (currencies differ): null targetAmountCents → Result.failure with descriptive error
- FX transfer (currencies differ): valid targetAmountCents → Result.success; transaction has currencyCode = fromAccount.currency and targetAmountCents set
Refactor SaveTransferUseCase.invoke() signature: add `fromAccount: Account`, `toAccount: Account`, `targetAmountCents: Long? = null`
Add validation: if (fromAccount.currency != toAccount.currency && targetAmountCents == null) return Result.failure(...)
Set transaction.currencyCode = fromAccount.currency, transaction.targetAmountCents = targetAmountCents (null for same-currency)
Run './gradlew :app:testDebugUnitTest' and confirm all tests pass
definition_of_done: SaveTransferUseCase accepts fromAccount and toAccount objects instead of bare IDs
Same-currency transfer: targetAmountCents is null on saved transaction
FX transfer with missing targetAmountCents returns Result.failure
FX transfer with valid targetAmountCents saves with correct currencyCode and targetAmountCents
Unit tests cover all three cases and pass
